enumNames: P – Hunting, fishing and illegal killing, including bycatch of non-target animals in fishing gear
Ke – Picking and collecting: including transplantation to gardens
H – Disturbance and traffic
Ku – Wear and erosion: wear of soil and bedrock, for example due to trampling, off-road driving, rock climbing or overgrazing

R – Construction (terrestrial): construction related to housing, livelihoods, transport, energy production and recreation, road construction, and earthworks and spoil dumping associated with construction
Ks – Extraction activities: extraction of soil and rock materials, mining activities

Pm – Changes in agricultural land: drainage, changes in cultivation practices, changes in livestock management (excluding abandonment of pastures), mechanisation, and changes in cultivated species (excluding pesticides)
Pr – Land clearance for agriculture: conversion of forests, mires and meadows into fields

N – Succession and closure of open habitats: closure of meadows, wooded pastures and forest pastures after cessation of grazing and mowing; afforestation and overgrowth of gravel pits and other open areas
M – Forest management activities
Mp – Changes in forest tree species composition: e.g. low proportion and decline of broadleaved trees and spruce encroachment in herb-rich forests
Mv – Scarcity of old-growth forests: low proportion and decline of old-growth forests and old trees
Mk – Scarcity of burn areas: low proportion and decline of burnt areas and other early stages of natural succession
Ml – Scarcity of deadwood: low amount and decline of decaying wood, dead and dying trees, branches and cavity trees
O – Drainage and peat extraction: including maintenance drainage and later impacts of earlier drainage such as overgrowth of open mires; excluding stream channel clearing

Vr – Water construction and engineering: e.g. hydropower plants, sawmill and mill dams, water regulation, dredging and channel clearing (including streams), structural alteration of shore zones (e.g. embankments), lowering of lake water levels, construction of artificial reservoirs, groundwater abstraction and use of springs, including impacts caused by lowering of the groundwater table
Kh – Chemical impacts: environmental toxins, pesticides, air and water pollution, oil spills, and nutrient-enriching deposition (excluding habitat overgrowth)
Re – Eutrophication of waters: increase in nutrient levels in aquatic environments caused by human activities
I – Climate change: warming, drought, increased precipitation, more frequent extreme weather events and their impacts
S – Stochastic factors: threats caused by random events when a population or distribution area is very small, including short-term climatic fluctuations
Kil – Competition: competition from other species
Ris – Hybridization: hybridization with other species
Kv – Large population fluctuations
U – Changes outside Finland: e.g. habitat changes in birds’ wintering areas or stopover sites during migration, and hunting/harvesting abroad
Vie – Impacts of alien species: competition, hybridization, diseases, predation, grazing, ecosystem changes
Muu – Other known cause/threat: a known cause/threat not included in defined categories
? – Cause/threat unknown
endangerment.subject: MKV.endangerment
endangerment.title: Endangerment
